title: A doctor once told her not to run. Now she’s taking on the Boston Marathon.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sydney Southern, 33, from Peabody, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after overcoming a giant cell tumor.
Her journey began in 2014 with surgery at Mass General Hospital, initially advised only to engage in low-impact activity.
Southern is running to raise funds for Mass General, grateful for the care received during her recovery and rehabilitation.

title: Cambridge pediatric doctor running fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Dr. Jennifer Leah Goetz is running her fifth Boston Marathon to support Massachusetts General Hospital patients.
Goetz, a Cambridge pediatric doctor, finds running has helped with her depression and become an essential part of her life.
She started running during her training at Massachusetts General Hospital, working with cancer patients in pediatrics and child psychiatry.

title: Brothers unite for Boston Marathon run honoring family, cancer losses
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin and Conor Nally are running the 130th Boston Marathon to honor family and friends affected by cancer.
Collin Nally began running after his father's passing in 2023 and completed his first Boston Marathon the same year.
In 2026, Conor Nally joined his brother's marathon efforts, inspired by shared experiences and personal loss.

title: Mass. has one of the best minor league ballparks in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Polar Park in Worcester ranked No. 6 among the best minor league ballparks in the U.S. by USA Today.
Designed by Larry Lucchino and Janet Marie Smith, Polar Park opened in 2021 and revitalized Worcester.
Smiley Ball, Worcester Red Sox mascot, ranked No. 3 in the 10 best minor league mascots in the U.S.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 26, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 7th Street Burger opens on Newbury Street; another location to open April in South Boston.
Far Out Ice Cream to open fourth location in Brighton, targeting April 19 opening.
Nirvana Tea House Cafe reopens in Millis with plant-based menu under new ownership.

title: State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted in deadly Woburn crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted for motor vehicle homicide after a 2023 crash that killed Angelo Schettino in Woburn.
Prosecutors allege Quigley was driving under the influence when his police cruiser struck a van carrying Schettino.
The Suffolk DA's office will handle Quigley's prosecution due to his role as a Middlesex County State Police detective.

title: Framingham’s iconic ‘castle’ hotel has entered a new era. Take a look inside.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The former Sheraton Framingham Hotel is now The Framingham Hotel Collection, featuring three Marriott brand hotels.
The Renaissance Framingham Hotel & Conference Center offers over 25,000 sq ft of meeting and event space.
New culinary options include Malone's Irish Pub & Social and Provisions Hearth & Kitchen for dining guests.

title: With travel concerns rising, are you rethinking flights?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Concerns over TSA staffing at Logan Airport are rising due to the partial Department of Homeland Security shutdown.
Union leaders warn potential flight delays at Logan Airport as staffing issues may worsen during the busy summer season.
Recent events, including a fatal crash at LaGuardia Airport, contribute to heightened airport safety concerns.

title: This Boston hotel is giving music lovers a reason to travel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at the Seaport launched an All Access Pass package providing concert tickets even for sold-out events.
Biometric system at Logan Airport allows TSA PreCheck users to pass through security by showing only their face.
Coastal Maine Botanical Garden in Boothbay named among America's best botanical gardens; opens for the season on May 1.

title: Tom Brady reveals that he has ‘explored a lot of different things’ about making an NFL comeback
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady, 48, is retired from the NFL but has explored the possibility of a comeback, considering league rules.
An NFL spokesperson stated Brady would need to divest his Raiders ownership to play due to league policies.
Brady remains active in football through Raiders ownership and broadcasting, while confirming happiness in retirement.
